cases.prompt: automate releases for this npm package
set up changesets
publish to npm from CI without tokens
configure npm trusted publishing
changesets or semantic-release for this monorepo?
cut a release with a changelog
add provenance to our npm publishes
version bump + tag + publish automation pls
our release workflow leaks an npm token, fix it
fix the package.json exports
bundle the library with tsdown
set up the CI test matrix
add coverage thresholds
audit our dependencies
protect the main branch
write the changelog entry for this PR
deploy the next.js app to vercel
set up automated releases for this single-package repo
expected_behavior: picks the right tool with rationale: Changesets for intentional human-written notes/monorepos, semantic-release for strict conventional-commit autonomous flows
configures npm trusted publishing (OIDC): id-token write permission, exact repo/workflow registration, npm >=11.5.1 and Node >=22.14 on the runner
states the bootstrap gotcha: the FIRST version of a package cannot be published via OIDC - one manual/token publish is needed before trusted publishing takes over
provenance is automatic under trusted publishing; no long-lived NPM_TOKEN anywhere

cases.prompt: migrate our NPM_TOKEN-based publish workflow to OIDC
expected_behavior: registers the trusted publisher with the exact workflow filename (case-sensitive) and warns that workflow_call indirection breaks the exact-match
keeps the old token active only during cutover, then revokes it and recommends account-level enforcement of trusted-publishing-only
verifies with a real publish or dry-run before deleting the token

cases.prompt: releases keep failing halfway - tag exists but npm publish failed
expected_behavior: diagnoses the partial-failure ordering (tag-before-publish) and makes the pipeline idempotent or reorders publish-then-tag
checks the usual suspects: OIDC config mismatch, npm/Node versions on the runner, staged-publishing gates
does not recommend force-deleting published tags (immutable-release principle)
